blocks.description: Program header comment block with program name, author (Capital Markets Risk & Compliance Division), version 3.1, regulatory references (Basel III CRR Art. 92, CCAR FR Y-14A, Dodd-Frank §165), and change log (v1.0–v3.1); SECTION 1 environment setup with OPTIONS (mprint, symbolgen, mlogic, nocenter, msglevel=i); LIBNAME definitions for risk (read-only exposures/PD/LGD), fin (read-only capital/balance sheet), comp (read-only regulatory limits), and out (output filing); SECTION 2 global %LET parameters for reporting period (report_date, report_qtr, filing_year, filing_quarter, run_id via %SYSFUNC, reg_rc), regulatory minimum thresholds (min_cet1_ratio=0.045, min_tier1_ratio=0.060, min_total_ratio=0.080, ccb_rate=0.025, gsib_rate=0.010), stress testing parameters (n_quarters=9, base/adv/sev_loss_rate), and 6 schedule identifiers (HC-R_PART1/2, FR_Y14A_SUMM/LOSS/PPNR, FR_Y14Q_SUPP)
%INCLUDE for regulatory_formats.sas shared regulatory format library for filing codes

%MACRO calc_rwa(asset_class=, approach=): calculates risk-weighted assets for a given asset class; %IF &approach=STANDARDIZED branch: DATA step applying fixed Basel III risk weights by rating_band via SELECT/WHEN (AAA/AA=0.20, A=0.50, BBB/BB=1.00, B/CCC/D=1.50, unrated=1.00), sovereign domestic preferential treatment (risk_weight=0.00), credit risk mitigation (CRM) adjustment using collateral_value, RWA = adjusted_exposure * risk_weight; %ELSE IRB branch: DATA step with MERGE of risk.exposures and risk.pd_lgd_models by counterparty_id, default PD=0.03/LGD=0.45 if missing, effective maturity adjustment (capped 0.70–1.50), Basel III asset correlation factor (R) formula, capital requirement (K) via PROBIT/PROBNORM approximation of IRB formula, EAD with credit conversion factors, RWA = 12.5 * K * EAD; PROC SQL NOPRINT for portfolio-level aggregation (n_exposures, total_exposure, total_rwa, avg_risk_weight) with SELECT INTO for &rwa_<asset>_total macro variable

%MACRO capital_ratios: computes CET1/Tier1/Total capital ratios against total RWA; PROC SQL NOPRINT with 4 SELECT INTO statements: CET1 capital via CASE WHEN summing CET1_POSITIVE minus CET1_DEDUCTION, Additional Tier 1 (AT1_INSTRUMENT minus AT1_DEDUCTION), Tier 2 (T2_INSTRUMENT minus T2_DEDUCTION), and total RWA from 4-way UNION ALL of rwa_*_summary tables; DATA step creating out.capital_ratios with OUTPUT for CET1/Tier1/Total ratios compared to regulatory minimums (&min_cet1_ratio/&min_tier1_ratio/&min_total_ratio), surplus_deficit calculation, breach_flag via IFC; %IF &ccb_rate>0 conditional for Capital Conservation Buffer ratio with combined minimum; %IF &gsib_rate>0 conditional for G-SIB surcharge ratio; DATA _null_ reading out.capital_ratios to store cet1_ratio/tier1_ratio/total_ratio as macro variables via CALL SYMPUTX

%MACRO stress_testing(scenario=): projects losses and capital impacts over 9 quarters; %IF/%ELSE IF for scenario-specific parameters: BASE (loss_rate=0.005, gdp_shock=-0.005, unemp_delta=0.002), ADVERSE (0.025, -0.035, 0.025), SEVERELY_ADVERSE (0.055, -0.080, 0.060); DATA step with 4 ARRAY declarations (qtr_loss, qtr_capital, qtr_rwa, cum_loss each dimensioned &n_quarters) reading from rwa_corporate_summary and rwa_retail_summary; %DO q=1 %TO &n_quarters loop computing quarterly loss projection with increasing severity via q_weight, cumulative loss accumulation, capital after losses, and RWA migration under stress; derived min_capital and min_ratio across horizon; PROC MEANS with N MEAN STD MIN MAX SUM for quarterly projections; PROC APPEND to out.stress_results

%MACRO generate_schedules: generates 6 regulatory filing schedules via %DO s=1 %TO &n_schedules loop; %IF for each schedule ID: HC-R_PART1 (PROC SQL aggregating fin.capital_components by component_type/name), HC-R_PART2 (PROC SQL combining 4 rwa_*_summary tables via UNION ALL), FR_Y14A_SUMM (PROC SQL selecting from out.capital_ratios), FR_Y14A_LOSS (PROC SQL aggregating out.stress_results by scenario with q1-q3 losses and worst ratio), FR_Y14A_PPNR (PROC SQL pivoting fin.balance_sheet PPNR line items by quarter), FR_Y14Q_SUPP (PROC SQL joining risk.exposures with risk.pd_lgd_models and risk.collateral); ODS PDF FILE output for each schedule with PROC PRINT including institution name and RSSD ID in TITLE3
SECTION 7 main program banner and %PUT pipeline start notes with run_id, report_date, and filing_year/filing_quarter
Step 1 heading and %calc_rwa invocation with asset_class=CORPORATE, approach=IRB
%IF &syserr > 0 error check after CORPORATE RWA: sets reg_rc=1, %PUT ERROR, %GOTO reg_exit
%calc_rwa invocation with asset_class=SOVEREIGN, approach=STANDARDIZED
%IF &syserr > 0 error check after SOVEREIGN RWA: sets reg_rc=2, %PUT ERROR, %GOTO reg_exit
%calc_rwa invocation with asset_class=RETAIL, approach=IRB
%IF &syserr > 0 error check after RETAIL RWA: sets reg_rc=3, %PUT ERROR, %GOTO reg_exit
%calc_rwa invocation with asset_class=EQUITY, approach=STANDARDIZED
%IF &syserr > 0 error check after EQUITY RWA: sets reg_rc=4, %PUT ERROR, %GOTO reg_exit

PROC SQL consolidating RWA results into out.rwa_summary via 4-way UNION ALL of work.rwa_corporate_summary, work.rwa_sovereign_summary, work.rwa_retail_summary, work.rwa_equity_summary ordered by asset_class; %PUT Step 1 complete note
Step 2 heading and %capital_ratios invocation to compute CET1/Tier1/Total capital adequacy ratios
%IF &syserr > 0 error check after capital ratio calculation: sets reg_rc=5, %PUT ERROR, %GOTO reg_exit; followed by %PUT Step 2 complete note
Step 3 heading for stress testing projections; PROC DATASETS deleting prior out.stress_results to clear previous run data before new stress scenario projections
%stress_testing invocation with scenario=BASE for baseline loss projection
%IF &syserr > 0 error check after BASE stress test: sets reg_rc=6, %PUT ERROR, %GOTO reg_exit
%stress_testing invocation with scenario=ADVERSE for adverse loss projection
%IF &syserr > 0 error check after ADVERSE stress test: sets reg_rc=7, %PUT ERROR, %GOTO reg_exit
%stress_testing invocation with scenario=SEVERELY_ADVERSE for severely adverse loss projection
%IF &syserr > 0 error check after SEVERELY_ADVERSE stress test: sets reg_rc=8, %PUT ERROR, %GOTO reg_exit; followed by %PUT Step 3 complete note
Step 4 heading and %generate_schedules invocation to create all 6 regulatory filing schedules
%IF &syserr > 0 error check after schedule generation: sets reg_rc=9, %PUT ERROR, %GOTO reg_exit; followed by %PUT Step 4 complete note

Step 5 cover page summary: PROC SQL creating out.filing_cover_page with run_id, filing_period, report_date, total_rwa, cet1/tier1/total ratios via INPUT, n_exposures subquery from out.rwa_summary, schedules_filed count, filing_timestamp via DATETIME(), and filing_status='PENDING_REVIEW'; driven by single-row fin.balance_sheet(obs=1)

Step 6 validation reports: three PROC PRINT statements — (1) out.capital_ratios showing ratio_name, ratio_value, regulatory_min, surplus_deficit, breach_flag with PERCENT format; (2) out.rwa_summary showing asset_class, approach, n_exposures, total_exposure, total_rwa, avg_risk_weight; (3) out.stress_results showing scenario_name, total_exposure, base_capital, min_capital, min_ratio, gdp_impact, unemp_impact; each with filing period TITLE/TITLE2

SECTION 8 error handling and completion: %reg_exit label; %IF &reg_rc NE 0 error branch with %PUT ERROR messages (return code, run ID), DATA step creating out.filing_errors with run_id, error_step, error_code, error_message, error_datetime; %ELSE success branch with %PUT NOTE completion messages (run ID, report date, filing period, CET1/Tier1/Total ratios, schedules filed), DATA _null_ writing completion_flag.txt with RUN_ID, STATUS=SUCCESS, FILING_PERIOD, ratios, SCHEDULES_FILED, and COMPLETION_TIME

SECTION 9 cleanup: PROC DATASETS deleting intermediate work datasets (rwa_corporate_std/irb/summary, rwa_sovereign_std/irb/summary, rwa_retail_std/irb/summary, rwa_equity_std/irb/summary, stress_base, stress_adverse, stress_severely_adverse)
Reset OPTIONS to nomprint/nomlogic/nosymbolgen and final %PUT completion messages with pipeline end timestamp via %SYSFUNC(datetime(), datetime20.) and final return code
